question.label: Escalation guide
question.name: What would make the escalation line clearer in practice for you?
question.description: Surfaces the concrete cues or examples Riley needs to know when to escalate versus batch questions.

answer: The runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.

assessment.note: Names a concrete gap in live-incident handoff details, which is a strong clarity deficit and a mild sign of hesitancy to engage confidently in incidents. [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'live incidents' practical handoff details worth drilling next session.

new_queue.label: Missing context
new_queue.name: What are you still waiting to understand before you change an architecture assumption?

new_queue.label: Support network
new_queue.name: Who would you want to pull in first when you need context fast?

new_queue.label: Next support
new_queue.name: What would make the next couple of weeks steadier for you?

history.answer_excerpt: I am comfortable reading the services, but I am less sure how decisions are really made here.
I would benefit from a clearer guide for what to escalate quickly versus what to batch.
The runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.

I can trace most request paths now, though I still miss historical context behind certain tradeoffs.
I have not been fully clear on who is on point for each domain when priorities conflict.
I am trying not to interrupt people, so sometimes I keep questions in a notes doc instead of asking.
